description:
4 fixture failures in fixture_matrix (closure_typed_captures, concurrency_ping_pong, higher_order, tail_recursion) when run via AOT path. Output is correct ('100000', '42', etc.) but the binary SIGBUSes after printing.

Exposed (not introduced) by fz-siu.6.3 (commit 72855317) which added 'aot' to these fixtures' # paths: line. The AOT codegen bug existed before; the test runner just hadn't been exercising it.

## Reproduction

cargo build && target/debug/fz build fixtures/tail_recursion.fz -o /tmp/tr
/tmp/tr           # prints 100000 then exits 138 (SIGBUS)

## Diagnosis

LLDB inspection of tail_recursion crash:

  frame #0 PC = 0x80aa0203f3  (bogus)
  frame #1 LR = fz_main_entry + 20

Crash site is fz_fn_4 (print's cont k_2_s4):
  +40: ldr x3, [x3, #0x18]    ; x3 = self+24 (outer_cont = halt_cl)
  +44: ldr x1, [x3, #0x10]    ; x1 = outer_cont+16 (halt-cont body addr)
  +64: br x1                  ; tail-branch to halt-cont body

x1 = 0x52800080aa0203f3 decodes as two valid arm64 instructions ('orr x19, xzr, x2' + 'mov w0, #4'). That's CODE bytes, not a function pointer. Either outer_cont points at a code region, or the singleton's +16 was overwritten.

## Working theory: stale heap pointers after Cheney GC

count(100000, 0) does 100K recursive tail-calls. Each Cranelift tail call may trigger frame allocations through count's body. fz-siu.8/.9/.11 introduced Cheney-copy GC at park time. When GC fires, it copies live heap objects to a new region and updates forwarding pointers — but only for objects reachable through tracked roots.

Tail-call args are passed in REGISTERS (Tail CC) and never live in any visible root set (no stack frame to walk, no parked_cont entry). So if main's cont closure was on-heap and got forwarded mid-execution, the cont pointer threaded through count's recursive tail-calls is stale. When count's base case reaches Term::Return and loads cont+16 to indirect-call, it reads garbage from the freed/repurposed memory.

The halt-cont singleton itself lives off-heap (static_closure_bufs Boxes) so it isn't moved. But main's cont closure (built by fz_alloc_closure → on-heap allocation) IS movable.

## Validation steps

1. Set hardware watchpoint on the cont closure ptr's destination memory after fz_alloc_closure returns; observe whether GC overwrites it during count's recursion.
2. Add an assert at every return_call_indirect site: if the loaded code_ptr's tag bits != 0 (it's not a heap-aligned pointer), abort.
3. Try running tail_recursion with count(100, 0) instead of count(100000, 0) — if GC threshold isn't reached, may pass.

## Fix paths

(A) GC-root-aware tail-call: extend the GC root walk to scan callee-saved registers in addition to parked_cont. Requires the AOT codegen to spill heap pointers at GC-trigger points.

(B) Pin cont closures off-heap. Treat them like the halt-cont singletons (in static_closure_bufs). Requires alloc-classification at codegen time and a separate off-heap arena for non-movable closures.

(C) Disable Cheney GC during native execution windows (run user code to halt/park, only GC at park boundaries). fz-siu.7.3 already moves GC to park-time — maybe a recursion-without-park triggers an unwanted GC.

Path (C) is the lightest investigation. Path (A) is the correct long-term answer.

## Out of scope until this lands

fz-ul4.27.22.7 (closure-arg spec specialization) is independent — works on JIT path. Don't gate it on kf2.
title: [BUG] AOT path: indirect call to tagged value at cont→halt-cont seam
